title: Choosing Cakes at a Bakery
prompt: Imagine you are at a bakery with many delicious cakes. Practice ordering your favorite cake and explaining why you want it.
hint: Use phrases like 'I want...', 'It has...', and describe the cake's features.
sample: I want the strawberry cake with pink cream because it looks sweet and delicious.

ja.prompt: たくさんのおいしいケーキがあるパン屋さんにいると想像してください。好きなケーキを注文し、なぜそれを選んだのか説明してください。
ja.hint: 'I want...', 'It has...' のようなフレーズを使い、ケーキの特徴を説明しましょう。
ja.sample: I want the strawberry cake with pink cream because it looks sweet and delicious.

zh.prompt: 想像你在一家有很多美味蛋糕的麵包店。練習點你最喜歡的蛋糕並說明你為什麼想要它。
zh.hint: 使用像 'I want...', 'It has...' 這樣的句型，描述蛋糕的特點。
zh.sample: I want the strawberry cake with pink cream because it looks sweet and delicious.

title: Making a Choice with Limited Options
prompt: You have too many choices and feel confused. Practice how to choose between two options by listening to your feelings and making a decision.
hint: Use expressions like 'I think I want...', 'My tummy says...', and 'I choose...'.
sample: I think I want the fruit tart. My tummy says yes, so I choose the fruit tart.

ja.prompt: 選択肢が多すぎて迷っています。自分の気持ちに耳を傾けて、2つの選択肢の中から選ぶ練習をしましょう。
ja.hint: 'I think I want...', 'My tummy says...', 'I choose...' のような表現を使いましょう。
ja.sample: I think I want the fruit tart. My tummy says yes, so I choose the fruit tart.

zh.prompt: 你有太多選擇感到困惑。練習如何聽從自己的感覺，在兩個選項中做出決定。
zh.hint: 使用像 'I think I want...', 'My tummy says...', 'I choose...' 這樣的表達。
zh.sample: I think I want the fruit tart. My tummy says yes, so I choose the fruit tart.

title: Safety at Home: Not Opening the Door for Strangers
prompt: Practice how to respond when someone unknown comes to your door. Explain why you should not open the door and how to check safely.
hint: Use phrases like 'We cannot open the door for people we don't know', 'Let's look through the peephole first', and 'Ask a secret question'.
sample: We cannot open the door for people we don't know. Let's look through the peephole first and ask a secret question.

ja.prompt: 知らない人がドアに来たときの対応を練習しましょう。なぜドアを開けてはいけないのか、安全に確認する方法を説明してください。
ja.hint: 'We cannot open the door for people we don't know', 'Let's look through the peephole first', 'Ask a secret question' のようなフレーズを使いましょう。
ja.sample: We cannot open the door for people we don't know. Let's look through the peephole first and ask a secret question.

zh.prompt: 練習當陌生人來敲門時如何回應。說明為什麼不應該開門，以及如何安全地先查看。
zh.hint: 使用像 'We cannot open the door for people we don't know', 'Let's look through the peephole first', 'Ask a secret question' 這樣的句子。
zh.sample: We cannot open the door for people we don't know. Let's look through the peephole first and ask a secret question.

title: Exploring Indian Culture
prompt: Describe your experience visiting India. Talk about the colors, spices, clothes, food, and cultural activities like yoga and meditation.
hint: Use descriptive words for colors and tastes, and explain cultural practices politely.
sample: India is colorful and sunny. I tried spicy curry and crunchy samosa. I also learned to do yoga and meditation.

ja.prompt: インドを訪れた経験を説明してください。色、スパイス、服、食べ物、ヨガや瞑想のような文化的な活動について話しましょう。
ja.hint: 色や味を表す言葉を使い、文化的な習慣を丁寧に説明しましょう。
ja.sample: India is colorful and sunny. I tried spicy curry and crunchy samosa. I also learned to do yoga and meditation.

zh.prompt: 描述你參觀印度的經驗。談談顏色、香料、服裝、食物，以及像瑜伽和冥想的文化活動。
zh.hint: 使用描述顏色和味道的詞彙，並禮貌地解釋文化習俗。
zh.sample: India is colorful and sunny. I tried spicy curry and crunchy samosa. I also learned to do yoga and meditation.

title: Apologizing and Making Up After a Mistake
prompt: Practice how to apologize sincerely after making a mistake and how to forgive others. Use polite and kind expressions.
hint: Use phrases like 'I'm sorry', 'I didn't mean to', 'Please forgive me', and 'It's okay'.
sample: I'm sorry for ruining your painting. I didn't mean to. Please forgive me. It's okay, let's fix it together.

ja.prompt: ミスをした後に誠実に謝り、相手を許す練習をしましょう。丁寧で優しい表現を使ってください。
ja.hint: 'I'm sorry', 'I didn't mean to', 'Please forgive me', 'It's okay' のようなフレーズを使いましょう。
ja.sample: I'm sorry for ruining your painting. I didn't mean to. Please forgive me. It's okay, let's fix it together.

zh.prompt: 練習在犯錯後如何真誠道歉，以及如何原諒別人。使用禮貌和友善的表達。
zh.hint: 使用像 'I'm sorry', 'I didn't mean to', 'Please forgive me', 'It's okay' 這樣的句子。
zh.sample: I'm sorry for ruining your painting. I didn't mean to. Please forgive me. It's okay, let's fix it together.

title: Importance of Sleep and Being On Time for School
prompt: Explain why it is important to sleep early and wake up on time for school. Practice apologizing for being late and promising to improve.
hint: Use phrases like 'If you sleep late, you will be tired', 'I'm sorry for being late', and 'I promise to sleep early'.
sample: If you sleep late, you will be tired and late for school. I'm sorry for being late today. I promise to sleep early tonight.

ja.prompt: なぜ早く寝て、学校に遅れずに起きることが大切か説明してください。遅刻したことを謝り、改善を約束する練習をしましょう。
ja.hint: 'If you sleep late, you will be tired', 'I'm sorry for being late', 'I promise to sleep early' のようなフレーズを使いましょう。
ja.sample: If you sleep late, you will be tired and late for school. I'm sorry for being late today. I promise to sleep early tonight.

zh.prompt: 說明為什麼早睡早起對上學很重要。練習為遲到道歉並承諾改進。
zh.hint: 使用像 'If you sleep late, you will be tired', 'I'm sorry for being late', 'I promise to sleep early' 這樣的句子。
zh.sample: If you sleep late, you will be tired and late for school. I'm sorry for being late today. I promise to sleep early tonight.
